pattern: Doubling is only useful once you fix an index to receive every operation, so track what every other position already contributes with prefix/suffix ORs and try each index as the sole target.
hints: All k doublings are always best spent on a single element rather than split across several -- splitting never beats concentrating the shift on one index.
For each index i, the answer if you shift only nums[i] is: (OR of everything before i) | (nums[i] << k) | (OR of everything after i).
Precompute a running prefix OR while you scan left to right, and a suffix OR array up front, so each index's candidate value is O(1) to build; take the maximum over all indices.

edge_inputs.description: smallest legal values with maximum k
shifting a middle element that already overlaps other bits set
all identical values -- any index is equally good
already-large leading value dwarfs the rest even unshifted

stress.description: large random array with random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 15)
    return [nums, k]
